data.title: Pony AI rallies after Jefferies says it is set up to be a long-term robotaxi winner
data.description:
Jefferies started off coverage on Pony AI with a Buy rating. The firm's overarching view is that China's robotaxi market is transitioning from pilot to scale, with a total addressable market expected to reach RMB52 billion ($7.4B) by 2030. 

Analyst Johnson Wan and his team think Pony AI (AI) stands out as a robotaxi winner due to the company's regulatory leadership position, cost-down roadmap, and full-stack capabilities across OEMs, TNCs, and system innovation. "We see it as best positioned to harness network effects and margin expansion as autonomous mobility scales, capturing 30% of the domestic market long term," advised Wan. 

Pony AI (NASDAQ:PONY) was noted to be at the forefront of robotaxi commercialization because it is the only company licensed for fully driverless, fare-charging operations across four Tier 1 cities and has demonstrated its capability in navigating China’s fragmented and stringent regulatory landscape. In addition, Pony AI (NASDAQ:PONY) was highlighted as having formed key joint ventures with Toyota (TM), BAIC, and GAC-Toyota to co-develop its Gen-7 robotaxi at sub-RMB300L cost and integrate with WeChat and Uber (UBER) to unlock massive user access. 

Jefferies assigned a price target of $32.80 to Pony AI (PONY).

Shares of Pony AI (PONY) were up 4.0% in early trading on Friday.

data.title: Marvell rises after Oppenheimer gets bullish on company after investor meetings
data.description:
Shares of Marvell Technology (NASDAQ:MRVL) rose about 3% premarket on Friday after analysts at Oppenheimer highlighted key takeaways after hosting investor meetings this week with the company's CFO Willem Meintjes and Senior Vice President of Investor Relations Ashish Saran.

The firm raised the price target on Marvell's stock to $115 from $95 and reiterated its Outperform rating on the shares.

"Tone was notably confident as mgmt. outlined gen-gen custom AI ASIC project continuity and sustained networking outperformance. All segments are expected to grow 10%+ in CY26 as CSP[cloud service providers]/hyperscalers deploy AI infrastructure and cyclical verticals inflect," said analysts led by Rick Schafer.

The analysts noted that Marvell's deep/broad data center, or DC, AI platform (optics, ASIC, switching, storage) taps a $95B DC Total Addressable Market, or TAM. More than half ($55B) custom ($40B XPU, $15B XPU-attach).

Marvell has secured over 20 custom project wins to-date, split 6 XPU, 14+ XPU attach, the analysts added. The analysts said that no revenue ‘air pocket’ in custom AI ASIC next year as they believe Trainium3 (T3) remains on track for early 2026 followed by T4.

The analysts see 2026 custom ASIC up with forecast CSP capex, at least 20% ( more than $2B), led by existing customers/projects (including T3). Initial Microsoft Maia contribution likely in late 2026, becoming a significant driver in 2027.

Marvell's management sees $20B DC connectivity TAM. The company is a dominant supplier of PAM4 DSP (400G/800G/1.6T) for scale-out. Overall, Marvell's $3B+ optics business boasts more than 50% Compound Annual Growth Rate, or CAGR, since the IPHI acquisition, which closed in 2021.

The analysts look for Marvell to use Teralynx low latency technology to win in the emerging scale up switching opportunity which could eventually rival scale out switch TAM. 

"Confusion surrounding continuity at top customer AWS [Amazon Web Services] has pressured the stock for much of CY25. We believe the MRVLAWS relationship remains very much intact and see shares re-rating higher as mgmt. executes and investor confidence grows. MRVL's diverse, structural growth led by share/content gains in DC AI," said Schafer and his team.

data.title: Rocket Lab rallies to another record high on contract with Japan Aerospace Exploration Agency
data.description:
Rocket Lab Corporation (NASDAQ:RKLB) signed a contract with Japan Aerospace Exploration Agency (JAXA) for two satellite launches at the company’s New Zealand launch complex.

The latest development for Rocket Lab (NASDAQ:RKLB), coming on the heels of a  multi-launch contract with Institute for Q-shu Pioneers of Space, is setting the company’s stock up for its ninth consecutive day in the green and another all-time high.

The first launch for JAXA on its Electron rocket will take place in December to deploy the agency’s Rapid Innovative payload demonstration SatellitE-4 spacecraft.

The second launch will occur in 2026 to deploy multiple satellites, cameras, and antenna as part of a JAXA-manifested rideshare.

The latest mission for JAXA adds to the more than two dozen dedicated missions booked by the Japanese space industry on Rocket Lab’s Electron launch vehicle through the end of the decade.

“These missions are a demonstration of Electron’s global importance - supporting the growth of Japan’s space industry with launch on a U.S. rocket from a New Zealand launch site – and we’re proud to be entrusted to deliver them,” Rocket Lab CEO Sir Peter Beck said in a statement.

Besides private satellite launches, Rocket Lab (NASDAQ:RKLB) is positioning itself to be selected as a contractor for the Golden Dome missile defense project, currently allocated with an initial budget of $25B.  The company recently acquired electro-optical and infrared sensor system developer Goest to facilitate Rocket Lab's status as a "disruptive prime contractor for next-generation defense initiatives.” 

Rocket Lab (RKLB) shares opened nearly 8% higher.

data.title: Wall Street opens in an upbeat mood even as U.S. shutdown drags on
data.description:
The stock market traded in the green on Friday, a day after Wall Street paused its rally as concerns rose about the length of the U.S. government shutdown.

S&P 500 (SP500) +0.2%, the Dow (DJI) +0.4%, and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) +0.2%.

Wall Street's major indexes struggled to keep up momentum on Thursday and ended lower, as the U.S. Senate failed to pass two funding bills to end the government shutdown. Meanwhile, the Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell did not comment on the economic outlook or monetary policy at a Fed event.

"Markets have struggled for momentum over the last 24 hours, as investors question how long this rally can persist, whilst concern grew about an extended government shutdown in the U.S. So that meant equities saw a modest pullback," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said. 

"If the shutdown does persist into next week, we won’t get the CPI release on Wednesday, which will leave us increasingly uncertain about the U.S. economy... For now at least, this lack of data means we’re flying blind to some extent on the U.S. economy, and we still don’t have the September payrolls report that was meant to come out a week ago," Allen added.

Investors might get the September retail inflation numbers despite the ongoing U.S. government shutdown, as the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics has reportedly recalled some workers to get the report out.

Investors will, however, look forward to the October Consumer Sentiment data slated to be released during market hours. The first reading for the current month is expected to show another decline to 54.0 from 55.1 in September and 58.2 in August.

"The Michigan consumer sentiment poll with the associated inflation expectations data is due—but as ever will be subject to partisan distortion," UBS' Paul Donovan said. 

There are a couple of Fed speakers lined up, including Chicago Fed President Austan Goolsbee and St. Louis Fed President Alberto Musalem.

data.title: Children's Place stock jumps after insider buying report
data.description:
The Children's Place (NASDAQ:PLCE) stock was surging after the specialty retailer reported insider buying worth ~$50K.

Shares were +13.45% to $8.18 on Friday after the opening bell.

CEO Muhammad Umair purchased 7,143 shares of common stock at a weighted average price of $7 per share.

Umair now owns 277,667 shares of direct ownership.

data.title: Northern Graphite completes $1.4M non-brokered private placement
data.description:
• Northern Graphite (TSXV:NGC:CA) announced on Friday the closing of its previously announced direct private offering under which it has issued 13.11M common shares of the company at a price of $0.11 per share for gross proceeds of $1.44M.
  • The company plans to use net proceeds to fund a feasibility study of Northern's planned battery anode material (BAM) in Baie-Comeau and cover working capital and corporate expenses.
  • The lead investment had been made by BMI group, signaling support for the company's mine-to-market approach.
  • The offered shares issued are subject to the statutory hold period of four months and one day from the date of issuance, expiring on February 10, 2026, in accordance with applicable canadian securities legislation. 
  • The company may issue up to an additional 6.88M offered shares under a follow-on closing under the private placement.

data.title: Rocket Lab secures multiple launches with Japan Aerospace Exploration Agency
data.description:
• Rocket Lab (NASDAQ:RKLB) has signed a direct contract for two dedicated Electron launches with the Japan Aerospace Exploration Agency (JAXA).
  • Launching from Rocket Lab Launch Complex 1 in New Zealand, the two Electron missions will deploy satellites for JAXA’s Innovative Satellite Technology Demonstration Program. The first launch, scheduled from December 2025, will deploy the agency’s RApid Innovative payload demonstration SatellitE-4 (RAISE-4) spacecraft, a single satellite that will demonstrate eight technologies developed by private companies, universities, and research institutions throughout Japan.
  • The second launch, scheduled for 2026, is a JAXA-manifested rideshare of eight separate spacecraft that includes educational small sats, an ocean monitoring satellite, a demonstration satellite for ultra-small multispectral cameras, and a deployable antenna that can be packed tightly using origami folding techniques and unfurled to 25 times its size.
  • RKLB +6.32% premarket to $70.62.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Andina Copper announces fully subscribed $3M private placement
data.description:
• Andina Copper (OTCQB:PMMCF) has launched a fully subscribed non-brokered private placement for gross proceeds of $3M.
  • The placement includes the issuance of 7.5M common shares at a price of $0.40 per share.
  • Proceeds will support exploration at the company’s two key projects: Cobrasco Copper-Molybdenum Project in Chocó, Colombia Piuquenes Copper-Gold Porphyry Project in San Juan, Argentina.
  • The anticipated closing date for the placement is October 31, 2025.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Quoin Pharmaceuticals announces private placement financing of up to $104.5M
data.description:
• Quoin Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:QNRX) on Friday said it has entered into a securities purchase agreement to raise up to $104.5 million in gross proceeds, including initial upfront funding of $16.5 million and up to an additional $88 million upon the potential cash exercise of accompanying warrants at the election of the investors.
  • The private placement was priced at a premium to the company’s prior day’s closing stock price.
  • Pursuant to terms of the securities purchase agreement, Quoin will issue an aggregate of 1.99M American Depository Shares and accompanying warrants to purchase up to an aggregate of 7.97M ADSs, at a combined purchase price of $8.25 per ADS and accompanying warrants, in accordance with the "Minimum Price" requirement as defined in the Nasdaq rules.
  • QNRX is +75.0% to $14.28.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: RBC downgrades Mosaic after surprise miss in phosphate output, stock tumbles
data.description:
RBC Capital Markets downgraded fertilizer maker Mosaic (NYSE:MOS) to sector perform on Friday after the company said its Q3 phosphate output of 1.7 million tonnes came in below the company's expectations due to mechanical issues.

Mosaic, which mines, produces, and distributes millions of tons of potash and phosphate products each year, fell 10% to $30.17 in early trading after the announcement. 

Mosaic's phosphate sales also came in at 1.6 million tons, trailing production due to shipment timing delays.

"We are disappointed in another surprise phosphate production cut and expect shares will be down significantly on the news," analysts said as they cut the price target to $30.00.

"We are concerned that phosphate operational issues may persist, and expect at least several quarters of strong operations may be needed to win back investor confidence."

However, analysts said they continue to see a potential upside case with Mosaic (NYSE:MOS) returning to nameplate phosphate production, which would allow the company to benefit from still-elevated phosphate prices, but this will require much better execution.

On the second-quarter earnings call, Mosaic discussed delays in asset health restoration activities within the phosphate segment.

While the company hit its production targets for several weeks after completing maintenance in August, unexpected mechanical issues at the Riverview sulfuric acid plant and utility interruptions at Bartow in mid-September led to a sharp drop in output.

As a result, third-quarter phosphate production volumes of approximately 1.7 million tons, while meaningfully higher than previous quarters, fell below management's expectations.

Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant Ratings, this company has a Buy rating with a score of 3.89 out of 5. YTD, the stock is trading up 36%, compared to the broader S&P 500 market, which is up 14.5%.
